message:
fix(lipgloss): harden truncate_line_ansi against incomplete ANSI escapes

Replace unwrap/expect calls in ANSI escape sequence parsing with safe
fallible patterns to prevent panics on malformed or truncated input:

- CSI sequences: guard chars.next() before entering param loop so an
  incomplete "\x1b[" (no final byte) gracefully returns instead of
  unwrapping None
- OSC/DCS/SOS/PM/APC sequences: replace peek-based ST detection with a
  prev_was_esc flag, avoiding a second unwrap on the backslash char and
  correctly handling edge cases where ESC appears at end of input
- Simple two-char escapes: wrap chars.next().expect() in if-let to
  tolerate a lone ESC at end-of-string

Regression tests added:
- test_truncate_line_ansi_handles_incomplete_csi_sequence
- test_truncate_line_ansi_handles_unterminated_string_escape

Also syncs .beads/issues.jsonl with latest issue state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
